title: BP Flags Up to $5 Billion in Low-Carbon Energy Impairments
content: BP expects to book $4 billion to $5 billion in fourth-quarter impairments, mainly tied to its low-carbon energy businesses, as it redirects spending toward oil and gas to boost returns under new leadership including Chair Albert Manifold. The company plans to sell its stake in solar power group Lightsource bp, has spun off its offshore wind business into joint venture Jera Nex BP, and abandoned plans to build a biofuels plant in Amsterdam along with hydrogen plants in Australia and Britain. Jera Nex BP, formed after BP committed hundreds of millions of dollars in 2021 with German utility EnBW to secure UK seabed rights, was not among winners at a British offshore wind power contract auction on Wednesday. Jera Nex BP declined to comment. These moves underscore BPâ€™s strategic pivot away from aggressive energy transition investments toward traditional hydrocarbons, signaling a slower pace for clean energy projects.
author: Analysts Account
